title: Colon
body_html:
Arterial supply of the large intestine

The table below illustrates the main vessels supplying the sections of the large intestine. It should be noted that the junnction of the proximal 2/3 and distal 1/3 of the colon is where the arterial supply switches from superior mesenteric to inferior mesenteric artery.

Part of large intestineMain vessel supplyingOrigin of vessel
Appendix & caecumileocolicSuperior mesenteric
Ascending colonright colicSuperior mesenteric
Transversemiddle colic
(left colic supplies distal 1/3)		Superior mesenteric
Descendingleft colicInferior mesenteric
Sigmoidsigmoid branchesInferior meseteric
Rectumsuperior rectal branchesinferior mesenteric

Diagram illustrating arterial supply of the colon

Image sourced from Wikipedia
